si.t: Preliminary release data have been edited but are subject to further NCES quality control procedures. Imputed data for nonresponding institutions are not included. These data are used for First Look (Preliminary Data) publications. Provisional release data have undergone all NCES data quality control procedures. Data are imputed for nonresponding institutions. These data are used for First Look (Provisional Data) publications. Final release data include revisions to the provisional release data that have been made by institutions during the subsequent data collection year. The final release data can be used when the most up to date data are required; however, these data may not match tables from the First Look reports based on preliminary and provisional data.

Variable list: Lists all variables in the table. Includes the variable name, variable label and the Data type, format and maximum field width of each variable. The data type describes a variable as either a numeric (N) or Alphanumeric (A) and the format of a variable defines a variable as either continuous (Cont) or categorical (Disc).
Long description or glossary definition of a variable.
This worksheet contains the code values for of all categorical variables in the data file. It also lists the frequency and percent distribution for each code value by variable.
This worksheet lists the sum, mean, minimum and maximum values of all continuous variables in the data file.
Unique identification number of the institution
Status of IC component when institution was migrated
Type of imputation method Institutional Characteristics
Status of completions component when institution was migrated
Status of 12-month enrollment component whe data collection closed
ID number of parent institution - 12-month enrollment
Type of imputation method - 12 month enrollment
Response status of the institution Fall Collection IC component
Imputation status of the institution - Institutional characteristics

Identifies Parent/child institutions for the Completions component Parent records includes data for their institution and other campuses/institutions (child institutions). The child institution record will not have data because it is included with the parent. The variable IDX_C contains the UNITID of the parent institution.

UNITID number of parent institution reporting Completions data IDX_C contains the UNITID number of the parent institution institution. This data field is used to link child institutions with the parent institution.

Type of imputation method used for imputing data for nonresponding institutions. Imputations methods include using reported data from the prior year; using reported data from another institution (Nearest Neighbor); and using Group median data from a similar group of institutions.
Status of 12-month Enrollment component when data collection closed

Identifies Parent/child institutions for the 12-month Enrollment component Parent records includes data for their institution and other campuses/institutions (child institutions). The child institution record will not have data because it is included with the parent. The variable IDX_EF contains the UNITID of the parent institution.

UNITID number of parent institution reporting 12-month enrollment data. IDX_E12 contains the UNITID number of the parent institution institution. This data field is used to link child institutions with the parent institution.
Parent record includes data from child campuses
Child record - all data reported with parent campus
Response status for Student Financial Aid survey
Status of Student Financial Aid Survey when data collection closed
Parent/child indicator Student Financial Aid survey
ID number of parent institution Student Financial Aid
Parent/child allocation factor - Student Financial Aid
Type of imputation method Student Financial Aid
Status of Graduation rate survey when data collection closed
UNITID of parent institution reporting Graduation Rates
Status of Graduation rate 200 survey when data collection closed
UNITID of parent institution reporting Graduation Rates 200
Status of admissions component when institution was migrated
Type of imputation method - Admissions component
Response status of institution for Human Resources (HR) component
Status of Human Resources (HR) component when data collection closed
ID of institution where data are reported for the Human Resources (HR) component
Type of Imputation method - Human Resources (HR) component
Does institution have 15 or more full-time employees
Does institution have a tenure system
Response status of institution - Fall enrollment
Status of Fall Enrollment survey when data collection closed
ID number of parent institution - Fall enrollment
Type of imputation method - Fall enrollment
Status residence of first-time first-year students
Status total entering class and retention rates
Status of Finance survey when data collection closed
ID number of parent institution for Finance survey
Identifies reporting standards GASB, FASB, or modified FASB(for-profit institutions) used to report finance data
Beginning date of fiscal year covered (all finance)
End date of fiscal year covered (all finance)
Clean Opinion GPFS from auditor (all finance)
Account for Pell grants as pass through transactions or as federal grant revenues to the institution (FASB institutions)?
Account for Pell grants as pass through transactions or as federal grant revenues to the institution (private-for-profit institutions)?
Are intercollegiate athletic expenses accounted for as auxiliary enterprises or treated as student services?
Type of business structure for tax purposes (private-for-profit, degree-granting institutions)
Status of Academic Library component when institution was migrated
UnitID of Parent institution - Academic Libraries
Type of imputation method - Academic Libraries

Identifies Parent/child institutions for the Student Financial Aid component Parent records includes data for their institution and other campuses/institutions (child institutions). The child institution record will not have data because it is included with the parent. The variable IDX_SFA contains the UNITID of the parent institution.

UNITID number of parent institution reporting Student Financial Aid data IDX_SFA contains the UNITID number of the parent institution institution. This data field is used to link child institutions with the parent institution.
SFA collection form type - identifies survey screens
Response status of the institution to the Graduation Rates component of the winter data collection
Status of survey when data collection was closed

Identifies Parent/child institutions for the Graduation Rates component Parent records includes data for their institution and other campuses/institutions (child institutions). The child institution record will not have data because it is included with the parent. The variable IDX_GR contains the UNITID of the parent institution.

UNITID number of parent institution reporting Graduation Rate data. IDX_GR contains the UNITID number of the parent institution institution. This data field is used to link child institutions with the parent institution.

For the Cohort year, Did your institution enroll any full-time first-time students? Cohort year=Academic year 2009-10 for 4-year institutions and 2012-13 for both 2-year and less-than-2-year institutions.

Identifies Parent/child institutions for the Graduation Rates 200 component Parent records includes data for their institution and other campuses/institutions (child institutions). The child institution record will not have data because it is included with the parent. The variable IDX_GR2 contains the UNITID of the parent institution.

UNITID number of parent institution reporting Graduation Rate 200 data. IDX_GR2 contains the UNITID number of the parent institution institution. This data field is used to link child institutions with the parent institution.
Response status of the institution Winter collection Admissions component
Response status of institution for Human Resource ( HR) component. HR include Employee by Assigned Position, Salaries, and Fall Staff.
Status of the Human Resource (HR) component when data collection closed. HR include Employee by Assigned Position, Salaries, and Fall Staff.

Identifies Parent/child institutions for the Human Resources component Parent records includes data for their institution and other campuses/institutions (child institutions). The child institution record will not have data because it is included with the parent. The variable IDX_HR contains the UNITID of the parent institution. HR include Employee by Assigned Position, Salaries, and Fall Staff.

UNITID number of parent institution reporting Human Resource (HR) data. IDX_HR contains the UNITID number of the parent institution institution. This data field is used to link child institutions with the parent institution. HR include Employee by Assigned Position, Salaries, and Fall Staff.
Response status for Employee by Assigned Position Survey
Response status to the Faculty Salary survey
Response status for Fall Staff Survey

Identifies Parent/child institutions for the Fall Enrollment component Parent records includes data for their institution and other campuses/institutions (child institutions). The child institution record will not have data because it is included with the parent. The variable IDX_EF contains the UNITID of the parent institution.

Part A, 99.0000 CIP, enrollment by racial/ethnic categories 1 - Part response 3 - Part nonresponse, not imputed 4 - Part nonresponse, imputed -2 - Item not applicable -7 - Part nonresponse, closed -9 - not active

Part B, enrollment summary by age (collected odd years only) 1 - Part response 3 - Part nonresponse, not imputed 4 - Part nonresponse, imputed -2 - Item not applicable -6 - Part not applicable -7 - Part nonresponse, closed -9 - not active

Part C, total-first time, first-year students, by residence (collected even years only) 1 - Part response 3 - Part nonresponse, not imputed -2 - Item not applicable -6 - Part not applicable -7 - Part nonresponse, closed -9 - not active

Identifies Parent/child institutions for the Finance component Parent records includes data for their institution and other campuses/institutions (child institutions). The child institution record will not have data because it is included with the parent. The variable IDX_F contains the UNITID of the parent institution.

UNITID number of parent institution reporting Finance data IDX_F contains the UNITID number of the parent institution institution. This data field is used to link child institutions with the parent institution.
Indicate which reporting (accounting) standards are used to prepare your financial statements
Fiscal calendar year: Beginning month and year for the 12-month fiscal year that this report covers
Fiscal calendar year: Ending month and year for the 12-month fiscal year that this report covers
Did your institution receive a clean opinion on its General Purpose Financial Statements from your auditor for the fiscal year noted above?

GASB Statement No. 34 offers three alternative reporting models for special-purpose governments like colleges and universities. Which model will be implemented by your institution? (Applicable to institutions using GASB)

Does your institution account for Pell grants as pass through transactions (a simple payment on the student's account) or as federal grant revenues to the institution?
Pell Grants: under FASB standards institutions have the option to report Pell grants as federal revenue and scholarships (or allowances) or as a pass-through transaction, essentially a payment on the student's account. The latter option is sometimes referred to as an agency transaction. Please indicate whether the institution accounts for Pell grants as pass-through payments or as federal revenue. If the institution does not award Pell grants, select the applicable option.
Does your institution account for Pell grants as pass through transactions (a simple payment on the student's account) or as federal grant revenues to the institution?

What type of business structure is the institution for tax purposes?
Private for-profit degree-granting institutions with a business structure of either C Corporation or a Limited Liability Company (LLC) are required to report Federal, and State and Local income tax expenditures.

Identifies Parent/child institutions for the Academic Libraries component Parent records includes data for their institution and other campuses/institutions (child institutions). The child institution record will not have data because it is included with the parent. The variable IDX_AL contains the UNITID of the parent institution.

UNITID number of parent institution reporting Academic Libraries data IDX_AL contains the UNITID number of the parent institution institution. This data field is used to link child institutions with the parent institution.

Identifies if institution has an academic library. Degree-granting institutions were asked to report total library expenditures on the Institutional characteristic header component during the registration process. If expenditures are greater than zero, institutions are determined to have an academic library, and therefore required to report academic library data.

Identifies institutions that may have been affected by natural disaster's such as hurricanes, tsunamis, fires, etc. This variable can be used to explain possible anomalies in the reporting of IPEDS data.
Parent record - includes data from branch campuses
Child record - data reported with parent campus
Undergraduate student financial and undergraduate military servicemembers/veteran's benefits are applicable
Undergraduate student financial and undergraduate and graduate military servicemembers/veteran's benefits are applicable
Graduate military servicemembers/veteran's benefits screens are applicable
Parent record - includes data from child campuses
Child record - data reported at parent campus
No, did not enroll full-time, first-time (undergraduate) students
No, did not offer programs at or below the baccalaureate level
No, institution was not in operation in cohort year
Partial child record - reports partial data and other data reported with parent campus
Child record - reports partial data but other data is included with entity that is not a postsecondary institution
Does not participate in intercollegiate athletics
File Documentation for the IPEDS response status data for all survey components, 2015-16

This variable contains the parent/child allocation factors for the Fall Enrollment component. The variable IDX_EF contains the unitid of the parent or child, depending on the record type, that defines the relationship of the parent to it's children. These allocation factors can be used to allocate enrollment data reported by the parent institution to the children institutions.
These factors were used when creating the enrollment variables used in the Data Feedback reports and the frequently used/derived variables in the data center.

This variable contains the parent/child allocation factors for the Finance component. The variable IDX_F contains the unitid of the parent or child, depending on the record type, that defines the relationship of the parent to it's children.
These factors were used when creating the derived financial variables for the Data Feedback reports and the frequently used/derived variables in the data center.

This variable contains the parent/child allocation factors for the Student Financial Aid component. The variable IDX_SFA contains the unitid of the parent or child, depending on the record type, that defines the relationship of the parent to it's children. These allocation factors can be used to allocate student financial data reported by the parent institution to the children institutions.
These factors were used when creating the financial aid variables used in the Data Feedback reports.

This variable contains the parent/child allocation factor for the Graduation Rates component. The variable IDX_GR2 contains the unitid of the parent or child, depending on the record type, that defines the relationship of the parent to it's children. These allocation factors can be used to allocate graduation rate data reported by the parent institution to the children institutions.
These factors were used when creating the graduation rate variables used in the Data Feedback reports and the frequently used/derived variables in the data center

This variable contains the parent/child allocation factors for the Completions component. The variable IDX_C contains the unitid of the parent or child, depending on the record type, that defines the relationship of the parent to it's children. These allocation factors can be used to allocate completions data reported by the parent institution to the children institutions.
These factors were used when creating the completion variables used in the Data Feedback reports and the frequently used/derived variables in the data center.

This variable contains the parent/child allocation factors for the 12-month Enrollment component. The variable IDX_E12 contains the unitid of the parent or child, depending on the record type, that defines the relationship of the parent to it's children. These allocation factors can be used to allocate enrollment data reported by the parent institution to the children institutions.
These factors were used when creating the enrollment variables used in the Data Feedback reports and the frequently used/derived variables in the data center.

This variable contains the parent/child allocation factor for the Graduation Rates component. The variable IDX_GR contains the unitid of the parent or child, depending on the record type, that defines the relationship of the parent to it's children. These allocation factors can be used to allocate graduation rate data reported by the parent institution to the children institutions.
These factors were used when creating the graduation rate variables used in the Data Feedback reports and the frequently used/derived variables in the data center

This variable contains the allocation factors for the Human Resources survey component. The variable IDX_HR contains the unitid of the parent or child, depending on the record type, that defines the relationship of the parent to it's children. These allocation factors can be used to allocate human resource data reported by the parent institution to the children institutions.
These factors were used when creating the human resource variables used in the Data Feedback reports and the frequently used/derived variables in the data center

This variable contains the parent/child allocation factors for the Academic Library component. The variable IDX_AL contains the unitid of the parent or child, depending on the record type, that defines the relationship of the parent to it's children. These allocation factors can be used to allocate academic library data reported by the parent institution to the children institutions.
These factors were used when creating the academic library variables used in the Data Feedback reports and the frequently used/derived variables in the data center.
Identifies institutions that submitted revised completions data for July 1, 2014 through June 30, 2015 using the IPEDS 2015-16 Prior Year Data Revision system
Identifies institutions that submitted revised 12-month enrollment data for 2014-15 using the IPEDS 2015-16 Prior Year Data Revision system
Identifies institutions that submitted revised 2015-16 institutional characteristics data using the 2015-16 Prior Year Data Revision system
Identifies institutions that submitted revised 2014-15 student financial aid data using the 2015-16 Prior Year Data Revision system
Identifies institutions that submitted revised graduation rate data, status date August 31, 2015, using the 2015-16 Prior Year Data Revision system
Identifies institutions that submitted revised 2015-16 admissions data using the 2015-16 Prior Year Data Revision system
Identifies institutions that submitted revised 2015-16 human resources data using the 2015-16 Prior Year Data Revision system
Identifies institutions that submitted revised 2015 fall enrollment data using the 2015-16 Prior Year Data Revision system
Identifies institutions that submitted revised finance data, fiscal year 2015 using the 2015-16 Prior Year Data Revision system
Identifies institutions that submitted revised academic library data, fiscal year 2015, using the 2015-16 Prior Year Data Revision system
Status of Outcome Measures component when data collection closed
UNITID of parent institution reporting Outcome Measures
OM flag 1 - inconsistent data with full-time first-time cohort, 2-year institutions
OM flag 2 - inconsistent data with bachelor's cohort, 4-year institutions
OM flag 3 - inconsistent data with full-time first-time cohort, 4-year institutions
OM flag 4 - no completers data reported
OM flag 5 - no transfer-out data reported
OM flag 6 - inconsistent first-time, full-time transfer-out data
Response status of the institution to the Outcome measures component of the winter data collection
Status of survey component when data collection was closed

Identifies Parent/child institutions for the Outcome Measures component Parent records includes data for their institution and other campuses/institutions (child institutions). The child institution record will not have data because it is included with the parent. The variable IDX_OM contains the UNITID of the parent institution.

UNITID number of parent institution reporting Graduation Rate data. IDX_OM contains the UNITID number of the parent institution reporting Outcome Measures. This data field is used to link child institutions with the parent institution.

This variable contains the allocation factors for the Outcome Measures component. The variable IDX_OM contains the unitid of the parent or child, depending on the record type, that defines the relationship of the parent to it's children for the Outcome Measures component. These allocation factors can be used to allocate outcome measure data reported by the parent institution to the children institutions.

Does institution have 15 or more full-time employees? FULL-TIME STAFF (EMPLOYEES) - The type of appointment at the snapshot date determines whether an employee is full time or part time. The employee's term of contract is not considered in making the determination of full or part time. Employees are counted only once even if employed multiple ways (e.g., a full-time employee who also teaches part time on a per course basis is counted only as full time). Also, a full-time employee who has an additional overload part-time appointment, contract, or course payment is counted as full time. (If Institution has less than 15 full-time employes the Fall staff survey is not applicable).

Does institution have a tenure system?
Does institution have a tenure system?
This variable is derived so that users can disaggregate those employees that are nontenured not on tenure track from those employees that are in an institution with no tenure system. Both EAP and Fall Staff components collect these data using 1 data field which makes it very difficult to produce tables on tenure. It is assumed that if all employees are reported in the nontenured not on track/no tenure system column/row, then the institution has no tenure system. If the institution reports at least 1 employee as tenured or nontenured, on tenure track then the institution has a tenure system. This variable can be used in the IPEDS DAS to produce tables on tenure status. See derivation below:
All employees, Tenured faculty (EAPTOT,EAPRECTP= 1102 ) > 0 or All employees, Non-tenured faculty, on tenure track (EAPTOT,EAPRECTP=1103) > 0 then TENURSYS=1 (Has tenure system). ELSE TENURSYS=0 (No tenure system)

Do all of the instructional faculty at your institution fall into any of the following categories? All are part time All are military personnel All contribute their services (e.g., member of a religious order) All teach pre-clinical or clinical medicine. If all instructional faculty fall into any of these categories the Faculty Salary survey is not applicable.

UNITID number of parent institution reporting Fall enrollment data IDX_EF contains the UNITID number of the parent institution institution. This data field is used to link child institutions with the parent institution.

If your institution participates in intercollegiate athletics, are the expenses accounted for as auxiliary enterprises or treated as student services?
Intercollegiate Athletics: according to NACUBO descriptions of functional expenses, intercollegiate athletics may be treated as auxiliary enterprises (if operated as an essentially self-supporting operation) or as student services (if the program is not operated as an essentially self-supporting operation). Please indicate whether your institution treats expenses for intercollegiate athletics as auxiliary enterprises, as student services, or in another functional category, or if the institution does not participate in intercollegiate athletics. This question is optional for the fiscal year 2005 collection.
Identifies institutions that submitted revised graduation rate 200 data, status date August 31, 2015, using the 2015-16 Prior Year Data Revision system
Identifies institutions that submitted revised outcome measures data, status date August 31, 2015, using the 2015-16 Prior Year Data Revision system

This variable identifies 4-year institutions that reported inconsistent data for the 2007 first-time, full-time cohort on the 2015 OM survey as compared with the 2007 bachelor's degree seeking cohort reported on the 2015 GR200 survey and the 2013 GR150 survey.
These institutions reported that they only had a bachelor's cohort and they also did not report any students receiving a degree below the bachelors on the 2013 GR150 survey. For institutions that only have a bachelor's cohort and do not grant associate degrees or certificates below the bachelor's, the number in the cohorts, the number of completers at 6-years and 8-years should be identical on both 2015 outcome measures and GR200 survey components. 477 institutions were identified as having this inconsistency and were notified and had the opportunity to correct their data in the 2015-16 prior year revision system. This variable identifies 171 institutions that are still not identical on both survey components.
The Data File Documentation (DFD) report describing these inconsistencies is available at http://nces.ed.gov/pubsearch/pubsinfo.asp?pubid=2018067

This variable identifies institutions that reported no completers for one or more of the four OM cohorts:
1 - First-time, full-time 2 - First-time, part-time 3 - Non-first-time, full-time 4 - Non-first-time, part-time
Originally 16 institutions reported no completers for one or more of the four cohorts and were notified and had the opportunity to correct their data in the 2015-16 prior year revision system. This variable identifies 11 institutions that still reported no completers for one or more of the four OM cohorts.
The Data File Documentation (DFD) report describing these inconsistencies is available at http://nces.ed.gov/pubsearch/pubsinfo.asp?pubid=2018067

This variable identifies institutions that reported having zero transfer-out students (i.e., subsequently enrolled in another institution) for all of the OM cohorts (first-time full-time, first-time part-time, non-first-time full-time, and non- first-time part-time) . This consistency check was done for institutions that reported enrollment unknown aggregated across all 4 cohorts was greater than or equal to 70.
Institutions that did not report transfer-out students and reported transfer-out data on previous GR150 surveys are not identified using this variable. (See description for OMFLG6GR - OM flag 6).
857 institutions were identified as not reporting any transfer-out data and were notified and had the opportunity to correct their data in the 2015-16 prior year revision system. This variable identifies 460 institutions that still reported no transfer-out data for all OM cohorts.
The Data File Documentation (DFD) report describing these inconsistencies is available at http://nces.ed.gov/pubsearch/pubsinfo.asp?pubid=2018067

This variable identifies institutions that reported having zero transfer-out students (i.e., subsequently enrolled in another institution) for all of the OM cohorts (first-time full-time, first-time part-time, non-first-time full-time, and non- first-time part-time) and reported transfer-out data on the 2013 GR 150 survey (4-year institutions) or the 2010 GR 150 survey (2-year institutions) . This consistency check was done for institutions that reported total enrollment unknown aggregated across all 4 cohorts was greater than or equal to 70.
149 institutions were identified as having this inconsistency and were notified and had the opportunity to correct their data in the 2015-16 prior year revision system. This variable identifies 57 institutions that still have this inconsistency.
The Data File Documentation (DFD) report describing these inconsistencies is available at http://nces.ed.gov/pubsearch/pubsinfo.asp?pubid=2018067
Not applicable, revised OM data not released

This file contains response status information for all survey components for every institution in the 2015-16 IPEDS universe. This file will determine institutions that have responded; institutions that did not respond and have imputed data; and survey applicability. It will also identify institutions whose data represents multiple campuses (parent/child reporting). Fall survey components include Institutional Characteristics, 2015-16, Completions 2014-15 and 12-month Enrollment and Instructional Activity, 2014-15. Winter survey components include Admissions, 2015-16, Student Financial Aid 2014-15, Graduation Rates 150 and Graduation Rates 200, August 31, 2015. Outcome Measures survey component was collected in 2015-16, but has not yet been released. Spring survey components include Fall Enrollment, 2015, Finance, Fiscal year 2015, Academic Libraries 2014-15, and Human Resources, 2015-16. Revised release 1 identified institutions that submitted revisions for Institutional Characteristics (Special learning opportunities, selected services, information on Veterans and service members and Undergraduate Disabilities) 2015-16 and/or the Completions and 12-month enrollment 2014-15 components. Revised release 2 identifies institutions that submitted revisions for the Admissions 2015-16, Student Financial 2014-15, Graduation Rates 150, August 31, 2015, Fall Enrollment, 2015, Finance, Fiscal year 2015, Academic Libraries 2014-15 and Human Resources, 2015-16. Revised release 3 identifies institutions that submitted revisions for the final GR200 2015 and Outcome Measure 2015 components.

This variable identifies 2-year institutions that reported inconsistent data for the 2007 first-time, full-time cohort on the 2015 OM survey as compared with the 2007 first-time, full-time cohort on the 2011 GR200 survey.
These institutions reported the number of first-time, full-time students from the 2007 cohort who completed with an award at 6-years on the 2015 OM survey that was less than the number of first-time, full-time students from the 2007 cohort who completed with an award within 150% of normal time on the 2011 GR200 survey component. The number of completers with awards in OM was expected to be greater than (or equal to) the GR200 completers. The time period to complete an associate's degree and/or certificate is six and 8 years on OM, and the time period to complete an associate's degree and/or certificate is 150% and 200% of normal time to complete (maximum of 3 or 4 years) on the GR200 survey, so completers reported on OM should be greater than completers reported on GR 200. 119 institutions were identified as having this inconsistency and were notified and had the opportunity to correct their data in the 2015-16 prior year revisions system. This variable identifies the 69 institutions that still have this inconsistency.
The Data File Documentation (DFD) report describing these inconsistencies is available at http://nces.ed.gov/pubsearch/pubsinfo.asp?pubid=2018067

This variable identifies 4-year institutions that reported inconsistent data for the 2007 first-time, full-time cohort on the 2015 OM survey as compared with the 2007 first-time, full-time cohort reported on the 2013 GR150 survey.
These 4-year institutions reported that they had an other than bachelor's degree-seeking cohort on the 2013 GR150 survey. Also included were institutions reporting only a bachelor's cohort and did report students receiving a degree below the bachelors. For these institutions the full-time, first-time cohort should have been the same on both GR and OM and the number of completers reported on 2013 GR150 should be less than or equal to the number of completers reported on OM. The time period to complete an associate's degree and/or certificate is six years on the OM survey, and the time period to complete an associate's degree and/or certificate is 150% of normal time to complete (maximum of 3 years) on the GR150 survey, so completers reported on OM should be greater than completers reported on GR 150. 205 institutions were identified as having this inconsistency and were notified and had the opportunity to correct their data in the 2015-16 prior year revision system. This variable identifies 94 institutions that still have this inconsistency.
The Data File Documentation (DFD) report describing these inconsistencies is available at http://nces.ed.gov/pubsearch/pubsinfo.asp?pubid=2018067
